body_txt:
Be it a biological mother in Mama Bear mode or a Team Mom trying to establish order in a Family of Choice or discipline the Team Kids , there's a tone of voice. It's full of reproach, authority, and command, but also capable of great compassion, and if raised in anger, it's often Anger Born of Worry . Use of a "Mom" voice can be an Establishing Character Moment for the Team Mom . However, one need not be the Team Mom to make use of a "Mom" voice.
Sometimes, the person delivering the "Mom" voice is not even a mother. Babysitters or team members in their teens are capable of this. The key to this trope is that it's not used between a mother and her own children. Then the effect is mundane and expected. In other cases, however, it is a powerful tool to show both the potential power this woman has and also her more nurturing qualities, even if she's delivering discipline or correcting bad behavior. It is notable that the older the woman, and the more experience she has with actual children, the more effective her "Mom" voice will be, particularly with women who are mothers and/or grandmothers. This is a big part of the reason no one can stand up to the Almighty Mom . When used for discipline, few characters can stand against the "Mom" voice. It's often accompanied by a stern look, often staring right into someone else's own eyes for maximum effect. In fact, it might even be prefaced with a request to look them in the eye. And a Civilized Threat may be issued in extreme cases. Certain phrases are common when a "Mom" voice is in use, such as referring to the object of a lecture as "Young man/lady", such expressions as "You should know better", "You're old enough to know better", "what did I just say?", and ending a gentle reprimand with "okay?". It's extra serious if it escalates to Counting to Three . Threats of spankings are also not uncommon, and may, in fact, be acted upon if the "Mom" voice proves insufficient. Of course, being a "Mom" voice, it can also be used to comfort and console just as much as it can be to deliver a Corrective Lecture , or Familial Chiding . While most of the people who use the "Mom" voice are women, it is not impossible to find male characters using a "Mom" voice, although male characters will more often use it sarcastically while teasing their peers. Compare and contrast "The Reason You Suck" Speech , which may or may not be delivered with concern for the recipient. Often overlaps with and is accompanied by a Full-Name Ultimatum .
